text: A formidable challenge in environmental catalysis is reduction of toxic nitrogen oxides (NOx) in engine after-treatment systems. Here, we show that atom trapping of Ru results in highly active and durable catalysts that consist of atomically dispersed Ru(II) species supported on ceria. These are anchored specifically to the (100) facets of ceria nanocrystals in a $Ru_1O_5$ configuration, which is square pyramidal, a configuration different from the typically seen for singly dispersed Pt and Pd species (trigonal bipyramid and square planar respectively). Catalysts of this $Ru_1/CeO_2$ composition exhibit a record-high catalytic activity for NO oxidation, exceeding all previously reported platinum and palladium-based single-atom NO oxidation catalysts, while being stable under various conditions, such as under both wet and hydrothermally-aged conditions up to 750 °C. The catalytic performance is unprecedented, as these single-atom Ru catalysts can store up to 4-fold more NOx than their platinum-based counter parts while using only 0.05-0.5 wt% Ru, and achieve the 100 °C light-off temperature for the whole NOx over the full concentration range. Using a multifaceted approach of in situ DRIFTS, mass spectrometry, and modulation-excitation (MOD) spectroscopy, we show that these Ru species serve as “shuttles” to oxidize nitric oxide to nitrites and nitrates and simultaneously act as nitrosyl precursors in this system. The $Ru_1/CeO_2$ catalyst also shows an outstanding CO-low-temperature activity for the selective reduction of NO by CO. Finally, the involvement of O V over the Ru-Ce interface in the catalytic cycle is evidenced by modulated excitation experiments and attributed to the crucial role of the heterointerface between the metallic and oxide components in enabling this heterogeneous redox reaction. These findings pave the way towards designing efficient dilute single-atom catalysts in a cost-effective manner for next generation catalysts required for reducing environmental harmful emissions.
